item.text: The validator is right on the work-packet side: I’ve found descriptor constants and runtime path helpers, but no workflow caller currently writes `work_packets/<wp_id>/packet.json` or `summary.json`. I’m checking existing storage accessors now so I can emit those files from `workflows.rs` without widening into storage code.

I’ve got the shape now. `TrackedMicroTask` really does lack a canonical top-level `updated_at`, and the only runtime work-packet support in scope today is descriptor/path plumbing. I’m reading the test harness and storage result payloads so I can add the missing emitters and direct projection tests without touching storage code.

269:fn sha256_hex(bytes: &[u8]) -> String {
270:    let mut hasher = Sha256::new();
271:    hasher.update(bytes);
272:    hex::encode(hasher.finalize())
273:}

275:fn model_swap_ref_to_abs_path(repo_root: &Path, state_ref: &str) -> PathBuf {
276:    let state_ref = state_ref.trim();
277:    let rel_path = if let Some(rest) = state_ref.strip_prefix("artifact:") {
278:        let mut parts = rest.splitn(2, ':');
279:        let _artifact_id = parts.next();
280:        parts.next().unwrap_or("")
281:    } else {
282:        state_ref
283:    };
284:    let rel_path = rel_path.strip_prefix('/').unwrap_or(rel_path);
285:    repo_root.join(rel_path)
286:}
287:
288:fn compute_model_swap_state_hash(
289:    repo_root: &Path,
290:    refs: &[String],
291:) -> Result<String, Box<dyn std::error::Error>> {
292:    let mut ref_hashes: Vec<(String, String)> = Vec::with_capacity(refs.len());
293:    for state_ref in refs {
294:        let abs_path = model_swap_ref_to_abs_path(repo_root, state_ref);
295:        let bytes = std::fs::read(&abs_path)?;
296:        ref_hashes.push((state_ref.clone(), sha256_hex(&bytes)));
297:    }
298:
299:    ref_hashes.sort_by(|(a, _), (b, _)| a.cmp(b));
300:    let mut manifest = String::new();
301:    for (state_ref, file_hash) in ref_hashes {
302:        manifest.push_str(&state_ref);
303:        manifest.push('\n');
304:        manifest.push_str(&file_hash);
305:        manifest.push('\n');
306:    }
307:
308:    Ok(sha256_hex(manifest.as_bytes()))
309:}

Handshake_Master_Spec_v02.178.md:62:| v02.168 | 2026-03-10 | Orchestrator | **Base structured schema and project-profile contract pass:** defined the shared base envelope, compact summary contract, and mirror-state semantics for structured collaboration artifacts; clarified project-profile extension boundaries for Work Packets, Micro-Tasks, Task Board projections, and Role Mailbox exports; strengthened Dev Command Center field provenance and summary ingestion rules; and deepened Appendix 12 ownership, coverage, and stub mapping for schema-registry and profile-extension implementation gaps. | ilja100320260150 |
Handshake_Master_Spec_v02.178.md:63:| v02.167 | 2026-03-10 | Orchestrator | **Canonical structured artifact backend pass:** defined the versioned JSON file standard for Work Packets, Micro-Tasks, Task Board projections, and Role Mailbox exports; clarified Markdown mirrors as readable derivatives plus note sidecars; added projected board and queue rules for Dev Command Center; introduced project-profile extensibility so structured collaboration artifacts do not stay repository-specific; and deepened Appendix 12 ownership, coverage, and stub mapping for the structured artifact family. | ilja100320260112 |

Handshake_Master_Spec_v02.178.md:72473:      "notes": "Operator surface to run governed development workflows, map work packets to worktrees and sessions, and review diffs. [ADD v02.144] Dev Command Center is the runtime projection surface for jobs, problems, timeline slices, tool calls, and recovery evidence. [ADD v02.147] Dev Command Center should round-trip export responses and capability and approval projection state instead of treating export actions as fire-and-forget. [ADD v02.159] Dev Command Center is the control and projection umbrella for Flight Recorder, bounded bundle launches, Role Mailbox projection, and worktree or session steering; Operator Consoles remain the specialized evidence and diagnostics views inside that umbrella. [ADD v02.160] Dev Command Center is also the governed control-plane projection for workflow runs, artificial intelligence job state, model-session scheduler state, effective capability snapshots, approval decisions, and work packet or worktree bindings; those backend artifacts MAY NOT remain hidden behind drawer-local state or console-only summaries. [ADD v02.161] Dev Command Center is also the governed evidence and replay projection for Governance Pack exports, Workspace Bundle exports, diagnostics query state, and bounded Debug Bundle validation outcomes; those export lifecycles MAY NOT live only inside console-local polling state or toast notifications. [ADD v02.162] Dev Command Center is also the governed work-orchestration projection for tracked Work Packet status, Task Board sync freshness, ready-query results, Micro-Task summaries, workflow-linked work packet activation, and parallel model session occupancy; those backend artifacts MAY NOT remain hidden behind kanban-only summaries, drawer-local routing state, or session-local heuristics. [ADD v02.163] Dev Command Center is also the governed planning-and-coordination projection for Task Board entries, Work Packet bindings, Spec Session Log continuity, and ready-work planning keyed by stable work-packet, workflow-run, micro-task, and model-session identifiers; those backend artifacts MAY NOT be reconstructed only from kanban ordering or packet-local prose. [ADD v02.164] Dev Command Center is also the governed resilience and repository-decision projection surface for session checkpoints, heartbeat freshness, provider capability readiness, anti-pattern alerts, and declared repository-engine backend policy including required status checks and merge-queue compatibility; those backend artifacts MAY NOT be guessed from console-local badges, adapter logs, or ad hoc git output. [ADD v02.165] Dev Command Center is also the governed operating surface for replay-safe run history, tool infrastructure health, workspace runtime readiness, and promotion-gate snapshots; those backend artifacts MAY NOT be inferred from visible transcript order, tool-list caches, workspace tabs, or optimistic repository badges. [ADD v02.166] Dev Command Center is also the structured record and collaboration-inbox viewing surface for Work Packet records, Micro-Task execution contracts, Task Board projection rows, append-only note timelines, and Role Mailbox triage state; typed fields and authoritative structured artifacts MUST remain primary over Markdown mirrors or transcript-only summaries. [ADD v02.167] Dev Command Center board, queue, roadmap, and Jira-like layouts are derived views over canonical structured collaboration artifacts, and operators MUST be able to inspect the exact authoritative fields and mirror-sync status behind every layout decision. [ADD v02.168] Dev Command Center also depends on a shared structured-collaboration base envelope, compact summary contract, and explicit profile-extension boundary so generic viewers and future project kernels can render the same records without software-delivery assumptions leaking into every view. [ADD v02.169] Dev Command Center is also the canonical mirror-reconciliation surface for authority mode, drift summary, regeneration action, and normalization posture; readable Markdown is never assumed safe when canonical or summary state disagrees. [ADD v02.170] Dev Command Center typed viewers also depend on explicit view presets, governed action bindings, and compact-summary-first execution or inbox queues so board, roadmap, triage, and local-small-model operating layouts can evolve without inventing hidden state transitions. [ADD v02.171] Dev Command Center also depends on one shared workflow-state family, queue-reason vocabulary, governed action descriptor set, and project-profile workflow label override contract so routing and action eligibility never collapse back into lane position or prose. [ADD v02.172] Dev Command Center also depends on explicit workflow transition rules, queue automation rules, and executor eligibility policies so every retry, reroute, review request, approval request, and automatic queue move can be previewed before authoritative state changes occur. [ADD v02.173] Dev Command Center also depends on typed Role Mailbox thread lifecycle, message delivery state, allowed-response envelopes, and mailbox-local-versus-governed action previews so inbox triage and Micro-Task collaboration loops remain queryable without transcript-order heuristics. [ADD v02.174] Dev Command Center also depends on mailbox-linked loop checkpoints, structured verifier outcomes, remaining retry budget, escalation targets, and completion-report transcription state so operator triage can resume, escalate, or close Micro-Task loops without reading full thread histories. [ADD v02.175] Dev Command Center also depends on mailbox triage queue state, reminder schedules, queue age, snooze or expiry posture, dead-letter disposition, and explicit operator remediation controls so queue aging, inbox pressure, and recovery actions remain inspectable before linked work changes. [ADD v02.176] Dev Command Center now also depends on mailbox executor-kind routing, claim mode, current claimant, lease expiry, takeover policy, and response-authority previews so operators can see who may act, who currently owns the thread, and whether takeover is legal before any reply or reroute occurs. [ADD v02.177] Dev Command Center now also depends on mailbox handoff bundle fields, announce-back provenance kind, recommended next actor, and transcription status so operators can resume, review, or close collaboration from compact structured state instead of replaying full threads.",
Handshake_Master_Spec_v02.178.md:72631:      "notes": "End-to-end tracking for Work Packets to Micro-Tasks with dependencies, sync, and event sourcing. [ADD v02.146] Status/query/result contracts must remain explicit so work tracking can be consumed deterministically by operator and model runtimes. [ADD v02.150] Workflow/node execution and task-board sync are backend projection contracts that keep ready-query, progress, and export scopes deterministic. [ADD v02.152] Locus Work Packet, Micro-Task, dependency, task-board, and query events are canonical Flight Recorder evidence seams; dedicated debug-bundle bridging remains stub-backed until explicit bundle-scope contracts land. [ADD v02.166] Locus canonical work-tracking state is structured-record-first for Work Packets, Micro-Tasks, dependencies, and Task Board projection rows; Markdown mirrors and long-form notes remain valuable but MUST stay derived, linked, and non-authoritative for routing and ready-work decisions. [ADD v02.167] Locus canonical collaboration records SHOULD use versioned JavaScript Object Notation envelopes with stable identifiers, compact summaries, and project-agnostic base fields so later project kernels do not inherit repository-specific mandatory fields. [ADD v02.168] Locus is also the shared schema-enforcement surface for the structured collaboration envelope, compact summary joins, and project-profile extension boundaries across Work Packets, Micro-Tasks, and Task Board rows. [ADD v02.169] Locus is also the desired-state reconciliation surface for Markdown mirrors and readable board projections; drift updates change mirror status and reconciliation action instead of mutating canonical packet state through prose. [ADD v02.171] Locus is also the authority for base `workflow_state_family`, `queue_reason_code`, and `allowed_action_ids` values so queues and future project kernels can relabel work without forking the underlying routing contract. [ADD v02.172] Locus is also the authority for portable workflow transition rules, automatic queue-move rules, and executor eligibility policies so queue grouping, retries, escalation, review, and approval are driven from durable backend law rather than board gestures or mailbox order. [ADD v02.173] Locus is also the authority that mailbox threads point at when a collaboration request might change linked work; thread lifecycle, due posture, and allowed responses may live in Role Mailbox, but authoritative packet or task state changes still resolve through Locus-backed governed actions or transcriptions. [ADD v02.174] Locus now also joins mailbox loop checkpoints, structured verifier outcomes, remaining retry budget, and completion-report transcription posture to authoritative Micro-Task and Work Packet state so waiting, retrying, escalated, and complete posture remains queryable without thread replay. [ADD v02.175] Locus now also joins mailbox triage queue state, reminder schedules, queue age, expiry posture, and dead-letter disposition to linked Work Packet, Micro-Task, and Task Board projections so mailbox pressure and remediation state remain queryable without relying on unread badges or thread order. [ADD v02.176] Locus now also joins mailbox executor kind, claim mode, current claimant, lease expiry, takeover policy, and response-authority scope to linked Work Packet, Micro-Task, and Task Board projections so temporary mailbox ownership stays queryable without implying that mailbox claims outrank governed work authority. [ADD v02.177] Locus now also joins latest accepted mailbox handoff bundle, announce-back provenance, and transcription-status summaries to linked Work Packet and Micro-Task records so takeover, resume, and review can rely on authoritative joins instead of mailbox chronology.",
Handshake_Master_Spec_v02.178.md:72808:      "notes": "Auto-generated Micro-Task decomposition plus iterative execution loop with escalation and validation reporting. [ADD v02.157] Pending distillation candidate queues, escalation-derived teacher/student lineage, and trust-tagged candidate evidence are part of the canonical backend learning surface, not optional helper state. [ADD v02.162] Micro-Task progress, hard-gate state, and session-occupancy bindings are canonical Dev Command Center work-orchestration projection inputs and MAY NOT live only in loop-local state, ad hoc progress polling, or isolated test counters. [ADD v02.166] Micro-Task definitions are also bounded structured execution contracts for local small models, with explicit execution tier, allowed tools, retry budget, escalation target, and expected output so delegation and handoff do not depend on packet-length Markdown ingestion. [ADD v02.167] Micro-Task canonical artifacts SHOULD support `packet.json` plus bounded `summary.json` outputs so local small models can read execution-ready fields without loading the full long-form reasoning trail. [ADD v02.168] Micro-Task records also inherit the shared base envelope and explicit profile-extension boundary so domain-specific execution hints do not pollute the generic local-small-model routing contract. [ADD v02.169] Micro-Task readable mirrors remain secondary to canonical execution contracts and summaries; advisory edits or long-form reasoning belong in note sidecars rather than backdoor task-state mutation. [ADD v02.170] Micro-Task execution queues SHOULD project explicit readiness buckets for local-small-model execution, human blockers, escalation, validation, and mailbox-response dependencies rather than relying on heuristic board placement. [ADD v02.171] Micro-Task routing SHOULD also declare one base workflow-state family, one queue reason code, and the currently allowed governed actions so local-small-model execution does not depend on board labels or mailbox ordering. [ADD v02.172] Micro-Task execution also depends on explicit transition rules, queue automation triggers, and executor eligibility policies so retry, escalation, validation, and mailbox-response waits do not silently change who may act next. [ADD v02.173] Micro-Task loops also depend on typed mailbox message families for request, feedback, verification, escalation, and completion reporting so Ralph-style verifier loops and bounded handoffs remain queryable without overloading generic blocker or status traffic. [ADD v02.174] Micro-Task loops now also publish bounded mailbox-loop checkpoints, structured verifier outcomes, remaining retry budget, and escalation posture so Role Mailbox, Work Packet notes, Task Board waits, and Dev Command Center queues can resume or audit loops without transcript replay. [ADD v02.176] Mailbox-linked Micro-Task loops now also surface current claimant, claimant kind, exclusive-versus-shared claim posture, lease expiry, and response-authority scope so execution queues can distinguish work that is truly free to resume from work that is still temporarily owned by another actor. [ADD v02.177] Mailbox-linked Micro-Task loops now also expose compact handoff bundle, announce-back provenance, and last transcription status so a resumed executor can see the safe next action without replaying verifier chatter. [ADD v02.178] Micro-Task loops default to `none`, `direct_load`, or `exact_lookup` retrieval modes and may use hybrid retrieval only for bounded discovery subtasks that are compacted before a local small model sees them."

30:/// Unified storage error type so callers don't leak provider-specific details.
31:#[derive(Debug, Error)]
32:pub enum StorageError {
33:    #[error("not found: {0}")]
34:    NotFound(&'static str),
35:    #[error("conflict: {0}")]
36:    Conflict(&'static str),
37:    #[error("validation failed: {0}")]
38:    Validation(&'static str),
39:    #[error("mutation guard blocked: {0}")]
40:    Guard(&'static str),
41:    #[error("not implemented: {0}")]
42:    NotImplemented(&'static str),
43:    #[error("serialization error: {0}")]
44:    Serialization(String),
45:    /// Opaque database error - hides provider-specific types [Â§2.3.12.3 Trait Purity]
46:    #[error("database error: {0}")]
47:    Database(String),
48:    /// Opaque migration error - hides provider-specific types [Â§2.3.12.3 Trait Purity]
49:    #[error("migration error: {0}")]
50:    Migration(String),
51:}
52:
53:// [Â§2.3.12.3] Manual From impl to convert sqlx::Error -> StorageError::Database
54:// This preserves the error message while hiding the sqlx type from public API.
55:impl From<sqlx::Error> for StorageError {
56:    fn from(err: sqlx::Error) -> Self {
57:        StorageError::Database(err.to_string())
58:    }
59:}
60:
61:// [Â§2.3.12.3] Manual From impl to convert MigrateError -> StorageError::Migration
62:// This preserves the error message while hiding the sqlx::migrate type from public API.
63:impl From<sqlx::migrate::MigrateError> for StorageError {
64:    fn from(err: sqlx::migrate::MigrateError) -> Self {
65:        StorageError::Migration(err.to_string())
66:    }
67:}
68:
69:impl From<serde_json::Error> for StorageError {
70:    fn from(value: serde_json::Error) -> Self {
71:        StorageError::Serialization(value.to_string())
72:    }
73:}

228:impl std::fmt::Display for ArtifactKind {
229:    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut std::fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> std::fmt::Result {
230:        match self {
231:            ArtifactKind::Log => write!(f, "log"),
232:            ArtifactKind::Result => write!(f, "result"),
233:            ArtifactKind::Evidence => write!(f, "evidence"),
234:            ArtifactKind::Cache => write!(f, "cache"),
235:            ArtifactKind::Checkpoint => write!(f, "checkpoint"),
236:        }
237:    }
238:}

item.text:
The remaining patch is scoped and concrete now:
1. add canonical `updated_at` to `TrackedMicroTask` and backfill/populate it in the registration path,
2. add real work-packet packet/summary emit + validate logic in `workflows.rs`, including task-board-driven status changes,
3. add direct integration coverage for work-packet and task-board structured artifacts, plus a deterministic task-board validation failure case.
Then I’ll reformat and run the strongest targeted verification the environment allows.

items.text: Patch micro-task registry model and registration/backfill to carry canonical updated_at
Add work-packet packet/summary emit+validate flow in workflows for WP-affecting Locus operations
Add direct work-packet and task-board structured artifact tests, then format and verify

item.text: The one test-harness risk I found is process-wide workspace-root env leakage across parallel integration tests. I’m tightening that now by serializing this test binary through a shared guard before I run verification, so the temp-workspace artifact tests stay deterministic instead of racing other `setup_state` cases.

item.text: The targeted cargo verification reached the same environmental wall as before: native DuckDB compilation fails before Rust can finish building the packet test targets. I’m doing one last source-level sanity pass now for export visibility and obvious compile traps, then I’ll report the exact commands and failure points cleanly.

item.aggregated_output:
2912:async fn execute_locus_work_packet_operation(
2913:    db: &dyn crate::storage::Database,
2914:    op: locus::LocusOperation,
2915:) -> Result<Value, WorkflowError> {
2916:    let runtime_paths = RuntimeGovernancePaths::resolve().map_err(|e| {
2917:        WorkflowError::Terminal(format!(
2918:            "failed to resolve runtime governance paths for work-packet registry: {e}"
2919:        ))
2920:    })?;
2921:    let (wp_id, kind_hint) = match &op {
2922:        locus::LocusOperation::CreateWp(params) => (params.wp_id.clone(), Some(params.kind)),
2923:        locus::LocusOperation::UpdateWp(params) => (params.wp_id.clone(), None),
2924:        locus::LocusOperation::GateWp(params) => (params.wp_id.clone(), None),
2925:        locus::LocusOperation::CloseWp(params) => (params.wp_id.clone(), None),
2926:        locus::LocusOperation::DeleteWp(params) => (params.wp_id.clone(), None),
2927:        _ => {
2928:            return Err(WorkflowError::Terminal(
2929:                "work-packet structured registry wrapper received non-work-packet operation"
2930:                    .to_string(),
2931:            ))
2932:        }
2933:    };
2934:    let payload = crate::storage::locus_sqlite::execute_locus_operation(db, op).await?;
2935:    emit_runtime_structured_work_packet_artifacts(&runtime_paths, db, &wp_id, kind_hint).await?;
2936:    Ok(payload)
2937:}
2938:
2939:async fn emit_runtime_structured_work_packet_artifacts(
2940:    runtime_paths: &RuntimeGovernancePaths,
2941:    db: &dyn crate::storage::Database,
2942:    wp_id: &str,
2943:    kind_hint: Option<locus::WorkPacketType>,
2944:) -> Result<(), WorkflowError> {
2945:    let workspace_root = runtime_paths.workspace_root().to_path_buf();
2946:    let packet_path = runtime_paths.work_packet_packet_path(wp_id).map_err(|e| {
2947:        WorkflowError::Terminal(format!("invalid work-packet packet path for {wp_id}: {e}"))
2948:    })?;
2949:    let summary_path = runtime_paths.work_packet_summary_path(wp_id).map_err(|e| {
2950:        WorkflowError::Terminal(format!("invalid work-packet summary path for {wp_id}: {e}"))
2951:    })?;
2952:    let tracked_wp =
2953:        load_tracked_work_packet_from_sqlite(runtime_paths, db, wp_id, kind_hint).await?;
2954:    let detail_value =
2955:        serde_json::to_value(&tracked_wp).map_err(|e| WorkflowError::Terminal(e.to_string()))?;
2956:    let summary = build_structured_work_packet_summary(&tracked_wp);
2957:    let summary_value =
2958:        serde_json::to_value(&summary).map_err(|e| WorkflowError::Terminal(e.to_string()))?;
2959:
2960:    let mut validation = locus::validate_structured_collaboration_record(
2961:        locus::StructuredCollaborationRecordFamily::WorkPacketPacket,
2962:        &detail_value,
2963:    );
2964:    let invalid_detail_authority_refs =
2965:        runtime_paths.invalid_runtime_authority_refs(&tracked_wp.authority_refs);
2966:    if !invalid_detail_authority_refs.is_empty() {
2967:        validation.push_issue(
2968:            locus::StructuredCollaborationValidationCode::AuthorityScopeMismatch,
2969:            "authority_refs",
2970:            Some(runtime_paths.governance_root_display()),
2971:            Some(invalid_detail_authority_refs.join(",")),
2972:            "work-packet authority_refs must stay within the product-runtime .handshake/gov boundary",
2973:        );
2974:    }
2975:
2976:    let mut summary_validation = locus::validate_structured_collaboration_summary_join(
2977:        locus::StructuredCollaborationRecordFamily::WorkPacketPacket,
2978:        &detail_value,
2979:        locus::StructuredCollaborationRecordFamily::WorkPacketSummary,
2980:        &summary_value,
2981:    );
2982:    let summary_authority_refs =
2983:        structured_collaboration_string_vec(summary_value.get("authority_refs"));
2984:    let invalid_summary_authority_refs =
2985:        runtime_paths.invalid_runtime_authority_refs(&summary_authority_refs);
2986:    if !invalid_summary_authority_refs.is_empty() {
2987:        summary_validation.push_issue(
2988:            locus::StructuredCollaborationValidationCode::AuthorityScopeMismatch,
2989:            "authority_refs",
2990:            Some(runtime_paths.governance_root_display()),
2991:            Some(invalid_summary_authority_refs.join(",")),
2992:            "work-packet summary authority_refs must stay within the product-runtime .handshake/gov boundary",
2993:        );
2994:    }
2995:    validation.merge(summary_validation);
2996:    if !validation.ok {
2997:        return Err(WorkflowError::Terminal(
2998:            serde_json::to_string(&validation).unwrap_or_else(|_| {
2999:                "structured collaboration validation failed for work-packet registry".to_string()
3000:            }),
3001:        ));
3002:    }
3003:
3004:    write_json_atomic(&workspace_root, &packet_path, &tracked_wp)?;
3005:    write_json_atomic(&workspace_root, &summary_path, &summary)?;
3006:    Ok(())
3007:}
3008:
3009:async fn load_tracked_work_packet_from_sqlite(
3010:    runtime_paths: &RuntimeGovernancePaths,
3011:    db: &dyn crate::storage::Database,
3012:    wp_id: &str,
3013:    kind_hint: Option<locus::WorkPacketType>,
3014:) -> Result<locus::TrackedWorkPacket, WorkflowError> {
3015:    let sqlite = sqlite_database_for_structured_work_packet_projection(db)?;
3016:    let row = sqlx::query_as::<
3017:        _,
3018:        (
3019:            String,
3020:            i64,
3021:            String,
3022:            String,
3023:            String,
3024:            i64,
3025:            String,
3026:            String,
3027:            Option<String>,
3028:            String,
3029:            Option<String>,
3030:            String,
3031:            String,
3032:            String,
3033:            String,
3034:            String,
3035:        ),
3036:    >(
3037:        r#"
3038:        SELECT
3039:            wp_id,
3040:            version,
3041:            title,
3042:            description,
3043:            status,
3044:            priority,
3045:            phase,
3046:            routing,
3047:            task_packet_path,
3048:            task_board_status,
3049:            assignee,
3050:            reporter,
3051:            created_at,
3052:            updated_at,
3053:            vector_clock,
3054:            metadata
3055:        FROM work_packets
3056:        WHERE wp_id = $1
3057:        "#,
3058:    )
3059:    .bind(wp_id)
3060:    .fetch_optional(sqlite.pool())
3061:    .await?;
3062:    let Some((
3063:        wp_id,
3064:        version,
3065:        title,
3066:        description,
3067:        status_raw,
3068:        priority,
3069:        phase_raw,
3070:        routing_raw,
3071:        task_packet_path,
3072:        task_board_status_raw,
3073:        assignee,
3074:        reporter,
3075:        created_at_raw,
3076:        updated_at_raw,
3077:        vector_clock_raw,
3078:        metadata_raw,
3079:    )) = row
3080:    else {
3081:        return Err(WorkflowError::from(StorageError::NotFound("work_packet")));
3082:    };
3083:
3084:    let mut metadata: Value =
3085:        serde_json::from_str(&metadata_raw).map_err(|e| WorkflowError::Terminal(e.to_string()))?;
3086:    if !metadata.is_object() {
3087:        metadata = json!({});
3088:    }
3089:
3090:    let packet_display = runtime_paths
3091:        .work_packet_packet_display(&wp_id)
3092:        .map_err(|e| {
3093:            WorkflowError::Terminal(format!(
3094:                "invalid work-packet packet display path for {wp_id}: {e}"
3095:            ))
3096:        })?;
3097:    let summary_display = runtime_paths
3098:        .work_packet_summary_display(&wp_id)
3099:        .map_err(|e| {
3100:            WorkflowError::Terminal(format!(
3101:                "invalid work-packet summary display path for {wp_id}: {e}"
3102:            ))
3103:        })?;
3104:    let existing_packet_kind = read_existing_work_packet_type(runtime_paths, &wp_id);
3105:    let kind = metadata
3106:        .get("work_packet_type")
3107:        .and_then(Value::as_str)
3108:        .and_then(parse_work_packet_type_value)
3109:        .or(kind_hint)
3110:        .or(existing_packet_kind)
3111:        .unwrap_or(locus::WorkPacketType::Feature);
3112:    let evidence_refs = task_packet_path
3113:        .clone()
3114:        .map(|path| vec![path])
3115:        .unwrap_or_default();
3116:
3117:    let labels = metadata
3118:        .get("labels")
3119:        .cloned()
3120:        .map(serde_json::from_value::<Vec<String>>)
3121:        .transpose()
3122:        .map_err(|e| WorkflowError::Terminal(e.to_string()))?
3123:        .unwrap_or_default();
3124:    let notes = metadata
3125:        .get("notes")
3126:        .cloned()
3127:        .map(serde_json::from_value::<Vec<locus::WorkNote>>)
3128:        .transpose()
3129:        .map_err(|e| WorkflowError::Terminal(e.to_string()))?
3130:        .unwrap_or_default();
3131:    let gates = metadata
3132:        .get("gates")
3133:        .cloned()
3134:        .map(serde_json::from_value::<locus::GateStatuses>)
3135:        .transpose()
3136:        .map_err(|e| WorkflowError::Terminal(e.to_string()))?
3137:        .unwrap_or_else(default_gate_statuses);
3138:    let started_at = parse_optional_timestamp_value(metadata.get("started_at"))?;
3139:    let completed_at = parse_optional_timestamp_value(metadata.get("completed_at"))?;
3140:    let due_at = parse_optional_timestamp_value(metadata.get("due_at"))?;
3141:    let profile_extension = metadata
3142:        .get("profile_extension")
3143:        .cloned()
3144:        .filter(|value| !value.is_null());
3145:    let tombstone = metadata
3146:        .get("tombstone")
3147:        .cloned()
3148:        .filter(|value| !value.is_null());
3149:
3150:    let mt_rows = sqlx::query_as::<_, (String, String)>(
3151:        "SELECT mt_id, status FROM micro_tasks WHERE wp_id = $1 ORDER BY mt_id ASC",
3152:    )
3153:    .bind(&wp_id)
3154:    .fetch_all(sqlite.pool())
3155:    .await?;
3156:    let mut micro_tasks = locus::MicroTaskSummary {
3157:        total: mt_rows.len() as u32,
3158:        completed: 0,
3159:        failed: 0,
3160:        in_progress: 0,
3161:        mt_ids: Vec::with_capacity(mt_rows.len()),
3162:    };
3163:    for (mt_id, mt_status_raw) in mt_rows {
3164:        micro_tasks.mt_ids.push(mt_id);
3165:        match parse_micro_task_status_value(&mt_status_raw)? {
3166:            locus::MicroTaskStatus::InProgress => {
3167:                micro_tasks.in_progress = micro_tasks.in_progress.saturating_add(1);
3168:            }
3169:            locus::MicroTaskStatus::Completed => {
3170:                micro_tasks.completed = micro_tasks.completed.saturating_add(1);
3171:            }
3172:            locus::MicroTaskStatus::Failed => {
3173:                micro_tasks.failed = micro_tasks.failed.saturating_add(1);
3174:            }
3175:            _ => {}
3176:        }
3177:    }
3178:
3179:    let mut tracked_wp = locus::TrackedWorkPacket {
3180:        schema_id: String::new(),
3181:        schema_version: String::new(),
3182:        record_id: String::new(),
3183:        record_kind: String::new(),
3184:        project_profile_kind: locus::ProjectProfileKind::SoftwareDelivery,
3185:        mirror_state: locus::MirrorSyncState::CanonicalOnly,
3186:        authority_refs: Vec::new(),
3187:        evidence_refs: Vec::new(),
3188:        summary_record_path: None,
3189:        profile_extension,
3190:        wp_id,
3191:        version: version.max(0) as u64,
3192:        title,
3193:        description,
3194:        status: parse_work_packet_status_value(&status_raw)?,
3195:        priority: priority.clamp(0, 4) as u8,
3196:        governance: locus::WorkPacketGovernance {
3197:            phase: parse_work_packet_phase_value(&phase_raw)?,
3198:            routing: parse_routing_policy_value(&routing_raw)?,
3199:            spec_session_id: metadata
3200:                .get("spec_session_id")
3201:                .and_then(Value::as_str)
3202:                .map(|value| value.to_string()),
3203:            gates,
3204:            task_packet_path,
3205:            task_board_status: parse_task_board_status_value(&task_board_status_raw)?,
3206:        },
3207:        kind,
3208:        labels,
3209:        assignee,
3210:        reporter,
3211:        micro_tasks,
3212:        created_at: parse_rfc3339_timestamp("created_at", &created_at_raw)?,
3213:        updated_at: parse_rfc3339_timestamp("updated_at", &updated_at_raw)?,
3214:        started_at,
3215:        completed_at,
3216:        due_at,
3217:        notes,
3218:        metadata,
3219:        vector_clock: serde_json::from_str(&vector_clock_raw)
3220:            .map_err(|e| WorkflowError::Terminal(e.to_string()))?,
3221:        tombstone,
3222:    };
3223:    locus::backfill_tracked_work_packet_registry_fields(
3224:        &mut tracked_wp,
3225:        summary_display,
3226:        vec![packet_display],
3227:        evidence_refs,
3228:    );
3229:    Ok(tracked_wp)
3230:}

3232:fn sqlite_database_for_structured_work_packet_projection(
3233:    db: &dyn crate::storage::Database,
3234:) -> Result<&crate::storage::sqlite::SqliteDatabase, WorkflowError> {
3235:    db.as_any()
3236:        .downcast_ref::<crate::storage::sqlite::SqliteDatabase>()
3237:        .ok_or_else(|| WorkflowError::from(StorageError::NotImplemented("locus sqlite")))
3238:}
3239:
3240:fn default_gate_statuses() -> locus::GateStatuses {
3241:    locus::GateStatuses {
3242:        pre_work: locus::GateStatus {
3243:            status: locus::GateStatusKind::Pending,
3244:            validated_at: None,
3245:            validated_by: None,
3246:            notes: None,
3247:            validation_report_ref: None,

3249:        post_work: locus::GateStatus {
3250:            status: locus::GateStatusKind::Pending,
3251:            validated_at: None,
3252:            validated_by: None,
3253:            notes: None,
3254:            validation_report_ref: None,

3256:    }
3257:}
3258:
3259:fn parse_optional_timestamp_value(
3260:    value: Option<&Value>,
3261:) -> Result<Option<DateTime<Utc>>, WorkflowError> {
3262:    match value {
3263:        None => Ok(None),
3264:        Some(Value::Null) => Ok(None),
3265:        Some(other) => serde_json::from_value::<DateTime<Utc>>(other.clone())
3266:            .map(Some)
3267:            .map_err(|e| WorkflowError::Terminal(e.to_string())),
3268:    }
3269:}
3270:
3271:fn parse_rfc3339_timestamp(field: &str, value: &str) -> Result<DateTime<Utc>, WorkflowError> {
3272:    DateTime::parse_from_rfc3339(value)
3273:        .map(|ts| ts.with_timezone(&Utc))
3274:        .map_err(|e| {
3275:            WorkflowError::Terminal(format!(
3276:                "invalid structured collaboration timestamp {field}={value}: {e}"
3277:            ))
3278:        })
3279:}
3280:
3281:fn parse_work_packet_status_value(value: &str) -> Result<locus::WorkPacketStatus, WorkflowError> {
3282:    serde_json::from_value(Value::String(value.to_string()))
3283:        .map_err(|e| WorkflowError::Terminal(format!("invalid work packet status {value}: {e}")))
3284:}
3285:
3286:fn parse_task_board_status_value(value: &str) -> Result<locus::TaskBoardStatus, WorkflowError> {
3287:    serde_json::from_value(Value::String(value.to_string()))
3288:        .map_err(|e| WorkflowError::Terminal(format!("invalid task board status {value}: {e}")))
3289:}
3290:
3291:fn parse_routing_policy_value(value: &str) -> Result<locus::RoutingPolicy, WorkflowError> {
3292:    serde_json::from_value(Value::String(value.to_string()))
3293:        .map_err(|e| WorkflowError::Terminal(format!("invalid routing policy {value}: {e}")))
3294:}
3295:
3296:fn parse_work_packet_phase_value(value: &str) -> Result<locus::WorkPacketPhase, WorkflowError> {
3297:    serde_json::from_value(Value::String(value.to_string()))
3298:        .map_err(|e| WorkflowError::Terminal(format!("invalid work packet phase {value}: {e}")))
3299:}
3300:
3301:fn parse_micro_task_status_value(value: &str) -> Result<locus::MicroTaskStatus, WorkflowError> {
3302:    serde_json::from_value(Value::String(value.to_string()))
3303:        .map_err(|e| WorkflowError::Terminal(format!("invalid micro-task status {value}: {e}")))
3304:}
3305:
3306:fn parse_work_packet_type_value(value: &str) -> Option<locus::WorkPacketType> {
3307:    serde_json::from_value(Value::String(value.to_string())).ok()
3308:}
3309:
3310:fn read_existing_work_packet_type(
3311:    runtime_paths: &RuntimeGovernancePaths,
3312:    wp_id: &str,
3313:) -> Option<locus::WorkPacketType> {
3314:    let packet_path = runtime_paths.work_packet_packet_path(wp_id).ok()?;
3315:    let packet_bytes = fs::read(packet_path).ok()?;
3316:    let packet_value: Value = serde_json::from_slice(&packet_bytes).ok()?;
3317:    packet_value
3318:        .get("type")
3319:        .and_then(Value::as_str)
3320:        .and_then(parse_work_packet_type_value)
3321:}
3322:
3323:fn build_structured_work_packet_summary(
3324:    tracked_wp: &locus::TrackedWorkPacket,
3325:) -> locus::StructuredCollaborationSummaryRecord {
3326:    locus::default_structured_collaboration_summary_record(
3327:        locus::StructuredCollaborationRecordFamily::WorkPacketPacket,
3328:        tracked_wp.record_id.clone(),
3329:        tracked_wp.title.clone(),
3330:        structured_work_packet_status(tracked_wp.status),
3331:        structured_work_packet_next_action(tracked_wp.status),
3332:        tracked_wp.authority_refs.clone(),
3333:        tracked_wp.evidence_refs.clone(),
3334:        tracked_wp.updated_at.to_rfc3339(),
3335:        tracked_wp.project_profile_kind,
3336:    )
3337:}
3338:
3339:fn structured_work_packet_status(status: locus::WorkPacketStatus) -> &'static str {
3340:    match status {
3341:        locus::WorkPacketStatus::Unknown => "stub",
3342:        locus::WorkPacketStatus::Ready => "ready",
3343:        locus::WorkPacketStatus::InProgress => "in_progress",
3344:        locus::WorkPacketStatus::Blocked => "blocked",
3345:        locus::WorkPacketStatus::Gated => "gated",
3346:        locus::WorkPacketStatus::Done => "done",
3347:        locus::WorkPacketStatus::Cancelled => "cancelled",
3348:    }
3349:}
3350:
3351:fn structured_work_packet_next_action(status: locus::WorkPacketStatus) -> &'static str {
3352:    match status {
3353:        locus::WorkPacketStatus::Unknown => "refine_work_packet",
3354:        locus::WorkPacketStatus::Ready => "start_work_packet",
3355:        locus::WorkPacketStatus::InProgress => "continue_work_packet",
3356:        locus::WorkPacketStatus::Blocked => "resolve_blocker",
3357:        locus::WorkPacketStatus::Gated => "await_gate_review",
3358:        locus::WorkPacketStatus::Done => "archive_work_packet",
3359:        locus::WorkPacketStatus::Cancelled => "close_work_packet",
3360:    }
3361:}

3364:    state: &AppState,

item.text:
**Checkpoint**

Resolved the three substantive validator findings in-scope.

This pass changed [types.rs](D:/Projects/LLM%20projects/Handshake/Handshake%20Worktrees/wt-WP-1-Structured-Collaboration-Schema-Registry-v1/src/backend/handshake_core/src/locus/types.rs#L323), [workflows.rs](D:/Projects/LLM%20projects/Handshake/Handshake%20Worktrees/wt-WP-1-Structured-Collaboration-Schema-Registry-v1/src/backend/handshake_core/src/workflows.rs#L2912), and [micro_task_executor_tests.rs](D:/Projects/LLM%20projects/Handshake/Handshake%20Worktrees/wt-WP-1-Structured-Collaboration-Schema-Registry-v1/src/backend/handshake_core/tests/micro_task_executor_tests.rs#L716). No Loom-protected path was touched.

`TrackedMicroTask` now has canonical top-level `updated_at`, it is populated/backfilled through the registration path, and the direct registration test now asserts it is present and RFC3339. Work-packet registry support is no longer descriptor-only: `workflows.rs` now emits and validates `work_packets/<wp_id>/packet.json` and `summary.json` on create/update/gate/close/delete, and `locus_sync_task_board_v1` also refreshes those work-packet artifacts after task-board-driven status changes. I also added direct task-board projection coverage: one success path for emitted `task_board/index.json` and `task_board/views/default.json`, and one deterministic authority-scope validation failure case.

**Verification**

- `rustfmt --edition 2021 src/backend/handshake_core/src/locus/types.rs src/backend/handshake_core/src/workflows.rs src/backend/handshake_core/tests/micro_task_executor_tests.rs src/backend/handshake_core/tests/role_mailbox_tests.rs src/backend/handshake_core/src/locus/task_board.rs src/backend/handshake_core/src/runtime_governance.rs src/backend/handshake_core/src/role_mailbox.rs src/backend/handshake_core/src/api/role_mailbox.rs`
  Result: passed.

- `git diff --check -- src/backend/handshake_core/src/locus/types.rs src/backend/handshake_core/src/workflows.rs src/backend/handshake_core/tests/micro_task_executor_tests.rs src/backend/handshake_core/tests/role_mailbox_tests.rs src/backend/handshake_core/src/locus/task_board.rs src/backend/handshake_core/src/runtime_governance.rs src/backend/handshake_core/src/role_mailbox.rs src/backend/handshake_core/src/api/role_mailbox.rs`
  Result: passed. Only Git CRLF warnings.

- `$env:CARGO_TARGET_DIR='D:\Projects\LLM projects\Handshake\Handshake Worktrees\wt-WP-1-Structured-Collaboration-Schema-Registry-v1\data\validator_followup_target_micro'; cargo test --test micro_task_executor_tests --no-run`
  Result: failed in native dependency build `libduckdb-sys v1.4.3`. Exact failure point was `cc-rs` invoking MSVC `cl.exe` on generated DuckDB C++ units under `...\data\validator_followup_target_micro\debug\build\libduckdb-sys-...\out\duckdb\...`, returning exit code `2`, before Rust could finish building the packet test target.

- `$env:CARGO_TARGET_DIR='D:\Projects\LLM projects\Handshake\Handshake Worktrees\wt-WP-1-Structured-Collaboration-Schema-Registry-v1\data\validator_followup_target_role'; cargo test --test role_mailbox_tests --no-run`
  Result: failed at the same environmental point: `libduckdb-sys v1.4.3` native MSVC build, before the Rust test target completed.

That blocker does not indicate a product-code defect in this WP diff because both commands fail inside the bundled DuckDB native dependency build before the touched Rust code can complete target compilation or execute tests.

**Remaining risk**

- Rust semantic verification is still limited by the `libduckdb-sys` MSVC build failure above.
- Legacy work packets created before this patch, with no existing emitted `packet.json`, still have one compatibility caveat: storage does not persist `WorkPacketType`, so reconstruction falls back to the create-op hint when available, then existing `packet.json`, else `feature`. New and freshly emitted packets are covered by the new workflow path.
